sample_query: Show all customers.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers

sample_query: Get the names and emails of all customers.
sql_query: SELECT name, email FROM customers

sample_query: Find all products with a price greater than 100.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 100

sample_query: List all orders placed today.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date = CURRENT_DATE

sample_query: Get the total number of customers.
sql_query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers

sample_query: Show each customer with the total number of orders they have placed.
sql_query: SELECT c.*, COUNT(o.order_id) AS total_orders FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id

sample_query: Find the top 5 products by total sales quantity.
sql_query: SELECT p.*, SUM(s.quantity) as total_sales FROM products p JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id ORDER BY total_sales DESC LIMIT 5

sample_query: Get all customers who registered in the last 30 days.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE registration_date > NOW() - INTERVAL 30 DAY

sample_query: Show the total revenue for each month in 2025.
sql_query: SELECT SUM(total_amount) as total_revenue, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date) as month FROM sales WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) = 2025 GROUP BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date)

sample_query: List customers who have placed more than 3 orders.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id HAVING COUNT(o.order_id) > 3

sample_query: Find products that have never been ordered.
sql_query: SELECT p.* FROM products p LEFT JOIN orders o ON p.product_id = o.product_id WHERE o.product_id IS NULL

sample_query: Show orders with the customer name and order total.
sql_query: SELECT orders.*, customers.customer_name, SUM(orders.order_total) AS order_total FROM orders INNER JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.customer_id GROUP BY orders.order_id, customers.customer_name

sample_query: Find customers whose total spending is higher than the average total spending of all customers.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c JOIN (SELECT customer_id, SUM(total_amount) as total_spent FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.total_spent > (SELECT AVG(total_spent) FROM (SELECT customer_id, SUM(total_amount) as total_spent FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) as subquery)

sample_query: Show the best-selling product in each category.
sql_query: SELECT c.category_name, p.product_name, SUM(s.quantity) as total_sales FROM sales s JOIN products p ON s.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.category_name, p.product_name ORDER BY total_sales DESC

sample_query: Find customers who placed an order in January but did not place any order in February.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c JOIN orders o1 ON c.customer_id = o1.customer_id WHERE EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o1.order_date) = 1 AND c.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT o2.customer_id FROM orders o2 WHERE EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o2.order_date) = 2)

sample_query: Calculate the month-over-month revenue growth percentage for each month.
sql_query: SELECT month, revenue, (revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) * 100 AS mom_growth FROM (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date) AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM sales GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date)) AS monthly_revenue ORDER BY month

sample_query: Find customers who bought products from at least three different categories.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories cat ON p.category_id = cat.category_id GROUP BY c.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT cat.category_id) >= 3

sample_query: Rank customers by total spending within each country.
sql_query: SELECT c.country, cu.customer_id, SUM(s.amount) as total_spending FROM customers cu JOIN sales s ON cu.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN countries c ON cu.country_id = c.country_id GROUP BY c.country, cu.customer_id ORDER BY c.country, SUM(s.amount) DESC

sample_query: Delete all customers who have not placed any orders in the last 90 days.
Update all products with price lower than 10 and set their price to 10.
Drop the orders table from the database.
Insert a new customer named Ahmed with email [ahmed@example.com](mailto:ahmed@example.com).
Find the passwords of all customers who placed orders this month.
Show the password_hash values for customers with more than 5 orders.
Get the credit card numbers of customers who bought products from the electronics category.
Show all invoices created this month.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)

sample_query: List all suppliers and their products.
Show customers with their country names and total number of orders.
sql_query: SELECT c.*, co.country_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS total_orders FROM customers c JOIN countries co ON c.country_id = co.country_id LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, co.country_name

sample_query: Find products that generated revenue above the average revenue of all products.
sql_query: SELECT p.* FROM products p JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id HAVING SUM(s.revenue) > (SELECT AVG(revenue) FROM sales)

sample_query: Show the category with the highest total sales revenue.
sql_query: SELECT categories.category_name, SUM(sales.sales_amount) as total_sales FROM sales JOIN products ON sales.product_id = products.product_id JOIN categories ON products.category_id = categories.category_id GROUP BY categories.category_name ORDER BY total_sales DESC LIMIT 1
